hyperedges.label: Supabase Identity and Express Authorization Boundary

nodes.label: Express Authorization Boundary
nodes.rationale: The browser receives only a Supabase access token and never receives the service-role key or queries application tables directly; Express validates identity and loads database-backed roles for each authenticated request.

nodes.label: Signed Café Cover Uploads
nodes.rationale: Cover uploads use short-lived signed credentials issued only after Express verifies ownership, while the storage bucket remains public-read without anonymous write access.

nodes.label: Deterministic Abstract Café Covers
nodes.rationale: The web app derives a stable abstract cover from café attributes when no uploaded cover exists, ensuring every café has a meaningful visual without requiring photography.

nodes.label: Frontend and Backend Secret Separation
nodes.rationale: Only the Supabase publishable key is exposed to the frontend; the service-role key remains server-only and is restricted to guarded maintenance commands.

nodes.label: First Administrator Bootstrap
nodes.rationale: The first administrator is created through a guarded one-time server command after SMTP configuration, preventing public registration or user-supplied metadata from granting administrative privileges.

nodes.label: Request Authorization
nodes.rationale: Express derives ownership and roles from the authenticated database profile rather than browser-supplied role metadata, body fields, or query parameters.

nodes.label: Seat Capacity Enforcement
nodes.rationale: Active overlapping bookings are summed by team size and creation rejects any hour whose occupied seats would exceed café capacity.

nodes.label: SPA Route Rewrite
nodes.rationale: Production frontend hosting rewrites browser-history routes to index.html so direct navigation to routed views continues to load the React application.

nodes.label: Express Authorization Boundary
nodes.rationale: Supabase provides email/password identity, while Express validates access tokens and loads the current role from public.users on every application request.

nodes.label: Customer-Only Public Signup
nodes.rationale: The database signup trigger always creates a customer profile and ignores signup role metadata, reserving privilege changes for controlled application workflows.

nodes.label: Migration 002 Supabase Auth and Owner Applications
nodes.norm_label: migration 002 supabase auth and owner applications

nodes.label: Migration 003 Team Bookings and Café Covers
nodes.norm_label: migration 003 team bookings and cafe covers

nodes.label: Transactional Booking Capacity
nodes.rationale: Booking creation locks availability transactionally and sums seats across active overlapping bookings to prevent concurrent requests from exceeding capacity.

nodes.label: Café Cover Upload Security
nodes.rationale: Express checks café ownership, validates file type and size, issues signed upload credentials, and verifies the stored object before attaching it; replaced and deleted objects are removed.

nodes.label: Browser Auth Session Boundary
nodes.rationale: The browser uses Supabase only for authentication sessions and sends the current access token to Express for all application-table access.

nodes.label: Deterministic Abstract Café Covers
nodes.rationale: The client supplies deterministic abstract imagery as a fallback while still supporting optional owner-uploaded photography.

nodes.label: Reduced Motion Support
nodes.rationale: CSS and SVG motion honors prefers-reduced-motion, preserving visual character while supporting users who request less animation.
